channel.title: Sedgewick, Robert on Chege's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Sedgewick, Robert on Chege's Blog

item.title: Shortest Paths in a Graph
item.description:
A shortest path from vertex \(s\) to vertex \(t\) in an edge-weighted digraph is a directed path from \(s\) to \(t\) with the property that no other path has a lower weight.

A shortest-paths tree for a source \(s\) is a subgraph containing \(s\) and all the vertices reachable from \(s\) that forms a directed tree rooted at \(s\) such that every tree path is a shortest path in the digraph.

item.title: Bloom Filters
item.description:
Bloom Filters

In true fashion to Stigler’s Law of Eponymy (no scientific discovery is named after its original discoverer ), Ross Ashby (1960) did a probabilistic analysis of Calvin Mooers’s Zatocoding (1947) (coding system for library cards). This predates Burton Bloom’s work (1970).

Motivation

A Bloom filter for a set \(X: \{x_1, x_2, …, x_n\} \) from some universe \(\mathbb{U}\) allows one to test whether a given item \(x \in \mathbb{B}\) is an element of \(X\). Hash tables can already answer \(x \in X\) queries in \(O(1)\) expected time, and \(O(n)\) space.
